texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 1:  FIFA WORLD CUP ™
texts.text: ARTICLE 1:  FIFA WORLD CUP ™

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.1 The FIFA World Cup™ is a FIFA competition embodied in the FIFA Statutes.
texts.text: 1.1 The FIFA World Cup™ is a FIFA competition embodied in the FIFA Statutes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.2 The FIFA World Cup takes place every four years. As a general rule, every association affiliated to FIFA may participate in the FIFA World Cup.
texts.text: 1.2 The FIFA World Cup takes place every four years. As a general rule, every association affiliated to FIFA may participate in the FIFA World Cup.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.3 Participation in the FIFA World Cup is free of charge.
texts.text: 1.3 Participation in the FIFA World Cup is free of charge.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.4 The FIFA World Cup 26™ consists of a preliminary competition and a final competition.
texts.text: 1.4 The FIFA World Cup 26™ consists of a preliminary competition and a final competition.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.5 The final competition ('FIFA World Cup 26') is scheduled to be played from 11 June to 19 July 2026.
texts.text: 1.5 The final competition ('FIFA World Cup 26') is scheduled to be played from 11 June to 19 July 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.6 Any rights associated with the FIFA World Cup 26 that are not granted by these Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ ('Regulations') and/or further regulations, guidelines and decisions as set forth in these Regulations and/or specific agreements to a Participating Member Association in the preliminary or final competition, or to a confederation, shall remain with and/or belong to FIFA.
texts.text: 1.6 Any rights associated with the FIFA World Cup 26 that are not granted by these Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ ('Regulations') and/or further regulations, guidelines and decisions as set forth in these Regulations and/or specific agreements to a Participating Member Association in the preliminary or final competition, or to a confederation, shall remain with and/or belong to FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.7 These  Regulations  govern  the  rights,  duties  and  responsibilities  of  all member associations taking part in the FIFA World Cup 26 ('Regulations'). The Regulations and all directives, decisions, guidelines and circulars issued by FIFA in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 are binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 1.7 These  Regulations  govern  the  rights,  duties  and  responsibilities  of  all member associations taking part in the FIFA World Cup 26 ('Regulations'). The Regulations and all directives, decisions, guidelines and circulars issued by FIFA in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 are binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.8 The FIFA Statutes and all FIFA regulations in force shall apply to and be binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26. Any reference in these Regulations to the FIFA Statutes and regulations refers to the FIFA Statutes and regulations valid at the time of application.
texts.text: 1.8 The FIFA Statutes and all FIFA regulations in force shall apply to and be binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26. Any reference in these Regulations to the FIFA Statutes and regulations refers to the FIFA Statutes and regulations valid at the time of application.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.9 Other rules and/or decisions that are directly binding on FIFA (e.g. due to FIFA being a signatory to the World Anti-Doping Code) may apply to the subject matter of these Regulations. Any such rules and/or decisions implemented by FIFA shall take precedence over these Regulations in the event of any conflict and shall be complied with by all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26™.
texts.text: 1.9 Other rules and/or decisions that are directly binding on FIFA (e.g. due to FIFA being a signatory to the World Anti-Doping Code) may apply to the subject matter of these Regulations. Any such rules and/or decisions implemented by FIFA shall take precedence over these Regulations in the event of any conflict and shall be complied with by all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26™.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 2:  PRELIMINARY COMPETITION
texts.text: ARTICLE 2:  PRELIMINARY COMPETITION

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.1 The preliminary competition shall be organised by FIFA in collaboration with the confederations. The confederations have established a competition format, which received the required approval from FIFA.
texts.text: 2.1 The preliminary competition shall be organised by FIFA in collaboration with the confederations. The confederations have established a competition format, which received the required approval from FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.2 On entering the preliminary competition, each member association:
texts.text: 2.2 On entering the preliminary competition, each member association:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  acknowledges and agrees that all administrative matters related to the preliminary competition shall be dealt with by the respective confederation in compliance with the regulations submitted, and FIFA will only intervene if a confederation requests FIFA's assistance in those matters; and
texts.text: acknowledges and agrees that all administrative matters related to the preliminary competition shall be dealt with by the respective confederation in compliance with the regulations submitted, and FIFA will only intervene if a confederation requests FIFA's assistance in those matters; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  undertakes to observe the principles of fair play.
texts.text: undertakes to observe the principles of fair play.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.3 The FIFA World Cup 26™ Play-Off Tournament ('Play-Off Tournament') is considered to be part of the preliminary competition. It is scheduled to be played within an existing window of the Men's International Match Calendar (23 to 31 March 2026). The Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ Play-Off Tournament are published by FIFA.
texts.text: 2.3 The FIFA World Cup 26™ Play-Off Tournament ('Play-Off Tournament') is considered to be part of the preliminary competition. It is scheduled to be played within an existing window of the Men's International Match Calendar (23 to 31 March 2026). The Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ Play-Off Tournament are published by FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.4 In the event that any of the confederations' preliminary competitions are not held or completed in a manner that determines which member association(s) qualify for the final competition (due to cancellation, postponement, abandonment or  otherwise),  FIFA  shall  decide,  after  consultation  with  the  applicable confederation, the member association(s) based within the territorial region of the confederation as the participating member association(s).
texts.text: 2.4 In the event that any of the confederations' preliminary competitions are not held or completed in a manner that determines which member association(s) qualify for the final competition (due to cancellation, postponement, abandonment or  otherwise),  FIFA  shall  decide,  after  consultation  with  the  applicable confederation, the member association(s) based within the territorial region of the confederation as the participating member association(s).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The confederation shall base its proposal strictly on the sporting merits of the member association(s) and shall provide a written report to FIFA explaining the reasons for proposing the member association(s) when seeking FIFA's approval. In the event that FIFA does not approve the confederation's proposed member association(s), the confederation shall propose an alternative member association(s) for FIFA's approval.
texts.text: The confederation shall base its proposal strictly on the sporting merits of the member association(s) and shall provide a written report to FIFA explaining the reasons for proposing the member association(s) when seeking FIFA's approval. In the event that FIFA does not approve the confederation's proposed member association(s), the confederation shall propose an alternative member association(s) for FIFA's approval.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 3:  FIFA ORGANISING BODY
texts.text: ARTICLE 3:  FIFA ORGANISING BODY

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.1 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee appointed by the FIFA Council is responsible for organising the FIFA World Cup 26 in accordance with these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations as well as the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.
texts.text: 3.1 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee appointed by the FIFA Council is responsible for organising the FIFA World Cup 26 in accordance with these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations as well as the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.2 The FIFA Council or the Bureau of the FIFA Council, may, if necessary, appoint a sub-committee to deal with unforeseen matters that require an immediate decision. Any decision taken by the Bureau of the FIFA Council, or by the sub-committee, shall come into effect immediately but shall be subject to confirmation by the FIFA Council.
texts.text: 3.2 The FIFA Council or the Bureau of the FIFA Council, may, if necessary, appoint a sub-committee to deal with unforeseen matters that require an immediate decision. Any decision taken by the Bureau of the FIFA Council, or by the sub-committee, shall come into effect immediately but shall be subject to confirmation by the FIFA Council.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.3 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee shall deal with any aspect of the FIFA World Cup 26 that is not the responsibility of any other body under the terms of these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations or the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.
texts.text: 3.3 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee shall deal with any aspect of the FIFA World Cup 26 that is not the responsibility of any other body under the terms of these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations or the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.4 The decisions taken by the FIFA Council or the relevant committee and/or its sub-committee in relation to the FIFA World Cup 26 may be appealed, pursuant to the FIFA Statutes.
texts.text: 3.4 The decisions taken by the FIFA Council or the relevant committee and/or its sub-committee in relation to the FIFA World Cup 26 may be appealed, pursuant to the FIFA Statutes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.5 FIFA bears overall responsibility for organising, hosting and staging the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 3.5 FIFA bears overall responsibility for organising, hosting and staging the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 4: LOCAL FIFA SUBSIDIARIES
texts.text: ARTICLE 4: LOCAL FIFA SUBSIDIARIES

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4.1 FIFA has established local FIFA subsidiaries, namely FWC26 Canada Football Ltd, FWC2026 Mexico S. de R.I de C.V and FWC2026 US, Inc. with offices based in Toronto, Canada, Mexico City, Mexico, and Miami, United States (each a 'Local FIFA Subsidiary'), in order to deliver various operational projects relating to the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 4.1 FIFA has established local FIFA subsidiaries, namely FWC26 Canada Football Ltd, FWC2026 Mexico S. de R.I de C.V and FWC2026 US, Inc. with offices based in Toronto, Canada, Mexico City, Mexico, and Miami, United States (each a 'Local FIFA Subsidiary'), in order to deliver various operational projects relating to the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4.2 Each Local FIFA Subsidiary may communicate with the Participating Member Associations from time to time on matters relating to the FIFA World Cup 26. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FIFA remains the ultimate authority and responsible party on all operational and competition matters relating to the organisation, hosting and staging of the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 4.2 Each Local FIFA Subsidiary may communicate with the Participating Member Associations from time to time on matters relating to the FIFA World Cup 26. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FIFA remains the ultimate authority and responsible party on all operational and competition matters relating to the organisation, hosting and staging of the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 5:  PARTICIPATING MEMBER ASSOCIATIONS - RESPONSIBILITIES
texts.text: ARTICLE 5:  PARTICIPATING MEMBER ASSOCIATIONS - RESPONSIBILITIES

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5.1 The  member  associations  that  qualify  for  the  FIFA  World  Cup  26 (the 'Participating Member Associations') automatically undertake to comply at all times with and ensure that every player, coach, manager, official, media officer, representative, guest and any other person carrying out duties on behalf of a Participating Member Association (each a 'Delegation Member') complies with these Regulations, The IFAB Laws of the Game, the FIFA Statutes and all other regulations issued by FIFA, including the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations, the FIFA Stadium Safety and Security Regulations, the FIFA Disciplinary Code, the FIFA Anti-Doping Regulations, the FIFA Code of Ethics and the FIFA Equipment Regulations, as well as with any other FIFA circular letters, regulations, guidelines, directives and/or decisions.
texts.text: 5.1 The  member  associations  that  qualify  for  the  FIFA  World  Cup  26 (the 'Participating Member Associations') automatically undertake to comply at all times with and ensure that every player, coach, manager, official, media officer, representative, guest and any other person carrying out duties on behalf of a Participating Member Association (each a 'Delegation Member') complies with these Regulations, The IFAB Laws of the Game, the FIFA Statutes and all other regulations issued by FIFA, including the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations, the FIFA Stadium Safety and Security Regulations, the FIFA Disciplinary Code, the FIFA Anti-Doping Regulations, the FIFA Code of Ethics and the FIFA Equipment Regulations, as well as with any other FIFA circular letters, regulations, guidelines, directives and/or decisions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition, each Participating Member Association shall, and shall ensure that every Delegation Member shall:
texts.text: In addition, each Participating Member Association shall, and shall ensure that every Delegation Member shall:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  respect the spirit of fair play, non-violence and non-discrimination and the authority of the match officials;
texts.text: respect the spirit of fair play, non-violence and non-discrimination and the authority of the match officials;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  behave accordingly; and
texts.text: behave accordingly; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  refrain from doping as defined by the FIFA Anti-Doping Regulations.
texts.text: refrain from doping as defined by the FIFA Anti-Doping Regulations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5.2 On entering the FIFA World Cup 26, the Participating Member Associations automatically undertake to:
texts.text: 5.2 On entering the FIFA World Cup 26, the Participating Member Associations automatically undertake to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  observe and comply with these Regulations and ensure that their Delegation Members also observe and comply with these Regulations and the principles of fair play;
texts.text: observe and comply with these Regulations and ensure that their Delegation Members also observe and comply with these Regulations and the principles of fair play;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  observe and comply with the FIFA Statutes, as well as with all other FIFA regulations, directives, circular letters, guidelines and decisions as well as all applicable laws;
texts.text: observe and comply with the FIFA Statutes, as well as with all other FIFA regulations, directives, circular letters, guidelines and decisions as well as all applicable laws;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  observe and comply with all instructions and decisions taken by the bodies and officials of FIFA under the terms of these Regulations and any other applicable FIFA regulations;
texts.text: observe and comply with all instructions and decisions taken by the bodies and officials of FIFA under the terms of these Regulations and any other applicable FIFA regulations;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d)  participate with the best possible team in all matches of the FIFA World Cup 26 in which their team is scheduled to take part;
texts.text: participate with the best possible team in all matches of the FIFA World Cup 26 in which their team is scheduled to take part;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e)  accept all of the arrangements relating to the FIFA World Cup 26 made by FIFA;
texts.text: accept all of the arrangements relating to the FIFA World Cup 26 made by FIFA;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f)  accept the use by FIFA and/or the issue of a sub-licence by FIFA to third parties, as well as the recording and broadcast of the images, names and records of
texts.text: accept the use by FIFA and/or the issue of a sub-licence by FIFA to third parties, as well as the recording and broadcast of the images, names and records of

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: General provisions I  .
texts.text: General provisions I  .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: all Delegation Members that may appear in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26; and
texts.text: all Delegation Members that may appear in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: g)  ensure the provision of adequate insurance to cover their Delegation Members and any other persons carrying out duties on their behalf against all risks, including but not limited to injury, accident, disease and travel arrangements in consideration of the relevant FIFA rules or regulations.
texts.text: ensure the provision of adequate insurance to cover their Delegation Members and any other persons carrying out duties on their behalf against all risks, including but not limited to injury, accident, disease and travel arrangements in consideration of the relevant FIFA rules or regulations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5.3 In addition, each Participating Member Association shall be responsible for:
texts.text: 5.3 In addition, each Participating Member Association shall be responsible for:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  the conduct of its Delegation Members;
texts.text: the conduct of its Delegation Members;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  paying for incidental costs and expenses incurred by its Delegation Members and any other persons carrying out duties on its behalf during the course of their stay in the host countries;
texts.text: paying for incidental costs and expenses incurred by its Delegation Members and any other persons carrying out duties on its behalf during the course of their stay in the host countries;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  paying for any costs of extending the stay, before or after the FIFA World Cup 26, of any Delegation Member or any other persons carrying out duties on its behalf;
texts.text: paying for any costs of extending the stay, before or after the FIFA World Cup 26, of any Delegation Member or any other persons carrying out duties on its behalf;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d)  applying for visas and submitting the required documents in good time in accordance with the relevant procedure; and
texts.text: applying for visas and submitting the required documents in good time in accordance with the relevant procedure; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e)  attending all media conferences and other media activities organised by FIFA in accordance with the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations, as well as with any other FIFA circular letters, guidelines, directives and/or decisions.
texts.text: attending all media conferences and other media activities organised by FIFA in accordance with the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations, as well as with any other FIFA circular letters, guidelines, directives and/or decisions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5.4 All Participating Member Associations shall confirm their participation in the FIFA World Cup 26 by submitting the entry form and any other required documentation as communicated by FIFA via the corresponding circular(s), duly signed by the deadline(s) set by FIFA. The timely submission to FIFA of any such documents is of the essence. If a Participating Member Association fails to adhere to the time limits and/or fails to meet the formal requirements for submitting the necessary documents, the FIFA organising body shall pass a decision.
texts.text: 5.4 All Participating Member Associations shall confirm their participation in the FIFA World Cup 26 by submitting the entry form and any other required documentation as communicated by FIFA via the corresponding circular(s), duly signed by the deadline(s) set by FIFA. The timely submission to FIFA of any such documents is of the essence. If a Participating Member Association fails to adhere to the time limits and/or fails to meet the formal requirements for submitting the necessary documents, the FIFA organising body shall pass a decision.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5.5 All Participating Member Associations shall indemnify, defend and hold FIFA, each Local FIFA Subsidiary, and all of their officers, directors, employees, representatives, agents and all other auxiliary persons free and harmless against  any  and  all  liabilities,  obligations,  losses,  damages,  penalties, claims, actions, fines and expenses (including reasonable legal expenses) of whatsoever kind or nature resulting from, arising out of, or attributable to, any non-compliance with these Regulations by the Participating Member Associations, their Delegation Members, their affiliates and any third parties contracted to the Participating Member Associations.
texts.text: 5.5 All Participating Member Associations shall indemnify, defend and hold FIFA, each Local FIFA Subsidiary, and all of their officers, directors, employees, representatives, agents and all other auxiliary persons free and harmless against  any  and  all  liabilities,  obligations,  losses,  damages,  penalties, claims, actions, fines and expenses (including reasonable legal expenses) of whatsoever kind or nature resulting from, arising out of, or attributable to, any non-compliance with these Regulations by the Participating Member Associations, their Delegation Members, their affiliates and any third parties contracted to the Participating Member Associations.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 6: WITHDRAWAL, UNPLAYED MATCHES, ABANDONED MATCHES AND REPLACEMENT
texts.text: ARTICLE 6: WITHDRAWAL, UNPLAYED MATCHES, ABANDONED MATCHES AND REPLACEMENT

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.1 All Participating Member Associations undertake to play all of their matches until eliminated from the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 6.1 All Participating Member Associations undertake to play all of their matches until eliminated from the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.2 Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 no later than 30 days before the first match of the final competition shall be fined at least CHF 250,000 by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee. Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 fewer than 30 days before the first match of the final competition shall be fined at least CHF 500,000 by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.
texts.text: 6.2 Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 no later than 30 days before the first match of the final competition shall be fined at least CHF 250,000 by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee. Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 fewer than 30 days before the first match of the final competition shall be fined at least CHF 500,000 by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Participating Member Associations that withdraw from the FIFA World Cup 26 at any time will be required to reimburse the team preparation money as well as any other tournament-related contribution payments that they received from FIFA.
texts.text: Participating Member Associations that withdraw from the FIFA World Cup 26 at any time will be required to reimburse the team preparation money as well as any other tournament-related contribution payments that they received from FIFA.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If any Participating Member Association is not admitted to, is excluded from or withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 once the tournament has started, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee may impose additional disciplinary measures. These may take into account, in particular, the timing of the withdrawal or exclusion, the severity of the rule breach leading to non-admission or exclusion, possible mitigating factors as well as any other relevant circumstances.
texts.text: If any Participating Member Association is not admitted to, is excluded from or withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 once the tournament has started, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee may impose additional disciplinary measures. These may take into account, in particular, the timing of the withdrawal or exclusion, the severity of the rule breach leading to non-admission or exclusion, possible mitigating factors as well as any other relevant circumstances.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Disciplinary sanctions may include the expulsion of the Participating Member Association  concerned  from  subsequent  FIFA  competitions  and/or  the replacement of the Participating Member Association with another member association. The FIFA Council or the relevant committee may decide, in particular, to replace the member association in question with another association.
texts.text: Disciplinary sanctions may include the expulsion of the Participating Member Association  concerned  from  subsequent  FIFA  competitions  and/or  the replacement of the Participating Member Association with another member association. The FIFA Council or the relevant committee may decide, in particular, to replace the member association in question with another association.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.3 Any match which is not played or which is abandoned - except in cases of force majeure recognised by FIFA - may lead to the imposition of disciplinary measures against the relevant Participating Member Association(s) by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 6.3 Any match which is not played or which is abandoned - except in cases of force majeure recognised by FIFA - may lead to the imposition of disciplinary measures against the relevant Participating Member Association(s) by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.4 Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 once the tournament has started or whose behaviour is liable for a match not being played or being abandoned may be ordered by the FIFA Council or the relevant committee to reimburse FIFA, the Local FIFA Subsidiary or any other Participating Member Association for any expenses incurred as a result of its behaviour. In such cases, the association concerned may also be ordered by FIFA to pay compensation for any damages incurred by FIFA, the Local FIFA Subsidiary or any other Participating Member Association. The association in
texts.text: 6.4 Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 once the tournament has started or whose behaviour is liable for a match not being played or being abandoned may be ordered by the FIFA Council or the relevant committee to reimburse FIFA, the Local FIFA Subsidiary or any other Participating Member Association for any expenses incurred as a result of its behaviour. In such cases, the association concerned may also be ordered by FIFA to pay compensation for any damages incurred by FIFA, the Local FIFA Subsidiary or any other Participating Member Association. The association in

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: General provisions I  .
texts.text: General provisions I  .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: question shall also forfeit any claim to financial remuneration from FIFA or the Local FIFA Subsidiary.
texts.text: question shall also forfeit any claim to financial remuneration from FIFA or the Local FIFA Subsidiary.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.5 If a Participating Member Association withdraws or a match cannot be played or is abandoned as a result of force majeure, the authorised FIFA organising body (including the Tournament Operation Centre) shall decide on the matter at its sole discretion and take whatever action is deemed necessary.
texts.text: 6.5 If a Participating Member Association withdraws or a match cannot be played or is abandoned as a result of force majeure, the authorised FIFA organising body (including the Tournament Operation Centre) shall decide on the matter at its sole discretion and take whatever action is deemed necessary.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.6 Further to the above provision, in the case of a match being abandoned as a result of force majeure after it has already kicked off, the following principles shall apply:
texts.text: 6.6 Further to the above provision, in the case of a match being abandoned as a result of force majeure after it has already kicked off, the following principles shall apply:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  The match shall recommence at the minute at which play was interrupted rather than being replayed in full, and with the same scoreline. The match shall recommence where play was stopped when the match was interrupted (e.g. with a free kick, throw-in, goal kick, corner kick, penalty kick, etc.). If the match was abandoned while the ball was still in play, it shall restart with a dropped ball from the position of the ball when play was stopped.
texts.text: The match shall recommence at the minute at which play was interrupted rather than being replayed in full, and with the same scoreline. The match shall recommence where play was stopped when the match was interrupted (e.g. with a free kick, throw-in, goal kick, corner kick, penalty kick, etc.). If the match was abandoned while the ball was still in play, it shall restart with a dropped ball from the position of the ball when play was stopped.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  The match shall recommence with the same players on the pitch and substitutes available as when the match was abandoned.
texts.text: The match shall recommence with the same players on the pitch and substitutes available as when the match was abandoned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  No additional substitutes may be added to the list of players on the start list.
texts.text: No additional substitutes may be added to the list of players on the start list.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d)  The teams can make only the number of substitutions to which they were still entitled when the match was abandoned.
texts.text: The teams can make only the number of substitutions to which they were still entitled when the match was abandoned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e)  Players sent off during the abandoned match cannot be replaced.
texts.text: Players sent off during the abandoned match cannot be replaced.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f)  Any disciplinary measures imposed before the match was abandoned will be dealt with in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: Any disciplinary measures imposed before the match was abandoned will be dealt with in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: g)  The kick-off time, date, location and any other matter shall be decided by FIFA.
texts.text: The kick-off time, date, location and any other matter shall be decided by FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.7 If any Participating Member Association withdraws and/or is excluded from the FIFA World Cup 26, FIFA shall decide on the matter at its sole discretion and take whatever action is deemed necessary. FIFA may decide to replace the Participating Member Association in question with another association.
texts.text: 6.7 If any Participating Member Association withdraws and/or is excluded from the FIFA World Cup 26, FIFA shall decide on the matter at its sole discretion and take whatever action is deemed necessary. FIFA may decide to replace the Participating Member Association in question with another association.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.8 If a Participating Member Association withdraws and/or is disqualified prior to the end of the group stage, the results of all of its matches will be declared null and void.
texts.text: 6.8 If a Participating Member Association withdraws and/or is disqualified prior to the end of the group stage, the results of all of its matches will be declared null and void.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.9 FIFA has the right to cancel, reschedule or relocate one or more matches (or the entire FIFA World Cup 26) for any reason at its sole discretion, including as a result of force majeure or due to health, safety or security concerns.
texts.text: 6.9 FIFA has the right to cancel, reschedule or relocate one or more matches (or the entire FIFA World Cup 26) for any reason at its sole discretion, including as a result of force majeure or due to health, safety or security concerns.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Disciplinary matters and procedures
texts.text: Disciplinary matters and procedures

texts.label: text
texts.orig: II
texts.text: II

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Disciplinary matters and procedures II  .
texts.text: Disciplinary matters and procedures II  .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 7:  DISCIPLINARY MATTERS
texts.text: ARTICLE 7:  DISCIPLINARY MATTERS

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7.1 Disciplinary infringements are dealt with in compliance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code in force as well as with all relevant circulars and directives, with which the Participating Member Associations and Delegation Members undertake to comply.
texts.text: 7.1 Disciplinary infringements are dealt with in compliance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code in force as well as with all relevant circulars and directives, with which the Participating Member Associations and Delegation Members undertake to comply.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7.2 FIFA may introduce new disciplinary rules and measures for the duration of the FIFA World Cup 26. Such rules shall be communicated to the Participating Member Associations before the first match of the final competition at the latest.
texts.text: 7.2 FIFA may introduce new disciplinary rules and measures for the duration of the FIFA World Cup 26. Such rules shall be communicated to the Participating Member Associations before the first match of the final competition at the latest.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7.3 Any violations of these Regulations or any other FIFA regulations, circulars, guidelines, directives and/or decisions that do not come under the jurisdiction of another FIFA body shall be dealt with by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 7.3 Any violations of these Regulations or any other FIFA regulations, circulars, guidelines, directives and/or decisions that do not come under the jurisdiction of another FIFA body shall be dealt with by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 8: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
texts.text: ARTICLE 8: DISPUTE RESOLUTION

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8.1 All disputes in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 shall be promptly settled by negotiation (with the exception of those falling under art. 7 above).
texts.text: 8.1 All disputes in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 shall be promptly settled by negotiation (with the exception of those falling under art. 7 above).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8.2 As provided for in the FIFA Statutes, Participating Member Associations and their Delegation Members may not take disputes to an ordinary court of law but to the exclusive jurisdiction of FIFA.
texts.text: 8.2 As provided for in the FIFA Statutes, Participating Member Associations and their Delegation Members may not take disputes to an ordinary court of law but to the exclusive jurisdiction of FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8.3 The  Participating  Member  Associations  and  all  Delegation  Members acknowledge and accept that, once all internal channels have been exhausted at  FIFA,  their  sole  legal  recourse  shall  be  to  the  Court  of  Arbitration  for Sport (CAS) in Lausanne, Switzerland, as provided for in the FIFA Statutes, and unless excluded by the FIFA Statutes or applicable FIFA regulations. Any such arbitration proceedings shall be governed by the CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration.
texts.text: 8.3 The  Participating  Member  Associations  and  all  Delegation  Members acknowledge and accept that, once all internal channels have been exhausted at  FIFA,  their  sole  legal  recourse  shall  be  to  the  Court  of  Arbitration  for Sport (CAS) in Lausanne, Switzerland, as provided for in the FIFA Statutes, and unless excluded by the FIFA Statutes or applicable FIFA regulations. Any such arbitration proceedings shall be governed by the CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 9: PROTESTS
texts.text: ARTICLE 9: PROTESTS

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.1 For the purpose of these Regulations, protests are objections of any kind related to events or matters that have a direct effect on matches, including but not limited to the state of and markings on the pitch, accessory match equipment, eligibility of players, stadium installations and footballs.
texts.text: 9.1 For the purpose of these Regulations, protests are objections of any kind related to events or matters that have a direct effect on matches, including but not limited to the state of and markings on the pitch, accessory match equipment, eligibility of players, stadium installations and footballs.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.2 Unless otherwise stipulated in this article, protests shall be submitted in writing to the FIFA Match Director within two hours of the match in question, as well as via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com (cf. art. 18 par. 1 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code) within 24 hours of the end of the match, otherwise they shall be disregarded.
texts.text: 9.2 Unless otherwise stipulated in this article, protests shall be submitted in writing to the FIFA Match Director within two hours of the match in question, as well as via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com (cf. art. 18 par. 1 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code) within 24 hours of the end of the match, otherwise they shall be disregarded.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.3 Protests regarding the eligibility of players nominated for matches shall be submitted via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com no later than five days before the first match of the final competition and shall be dealt with by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.
texts.text: 9.3 Protests regarding the eligibility of players nominated for matches shall be submitted via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com no later than five days before the first match of the final competition and shall be dealt with by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.4 Protests regarding the state of the pitch, its surroundings, markings or accessory items (e.g. goals, flag posts or footballs) shall be made in writing to the referee before the start of the match by the head of delegation of the team lodging the protest. If the pitch's playing surface becomes unplayable during a match, the captain of the protesting team shall immediately lodge a protest with the referee in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protests shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of the team delegation no later than two hours after the match.
texts.text: 9.4 Protests regarding the state of the pitch, its surroundings, markings or accessory items (e.g. goals, flag posts or footballs) shall be made in writing to the referee before the start of the match by the head of delegation of the team lodging the protest. If the pitch's playing surface becomes unplayable during a match, the captain of the protesting team shall immediately lodge a protest with the referee in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protests shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of the team delegation no later than two hours after the match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.5 Protests against any incidents that occur during the course of a match shall be made to the referee by the team captain immediately after the disputed incident and before play has resumed, in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protest shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of delegation no later than two hours after the match.
texts.text: 9.5 Protests against any incidents that occur during the course of a match shall be made to the referee by the team captain immediately after the disputed incident and before play has resumed, in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protest shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of delegation no later than two hours after the match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.6 No protests may be made about the referee's decisions regarding facts connected with play. Such decisions are final and not subject to appeal, unless otherwise stipulated in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 9.6 No protests may be made about the referee's decisions regarding facts connected with play. Such decisions are final and not subject to appeal, unless otherwise stipulated in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.7 If  an  unfounded or irresponsible protest is lodged, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee may impose disciplinary measures against the Participating Member Association concerned.
texts.text: 9.7 If  an  unfounded or irresponsible protest is lodged, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee may impose disciplinary measures against the Participating Member Association concerned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.8 If  any of the formal conditions of a protest as set out in these Regulations (and in the FIFA Disciplinary Code, where applicable) are not met, such protest shall be disregarded by the competent body. Once the final match of the FIFA World Cup 26 has ended, any protests described in this article shall be disregarded. Notwithstanding the above, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee remains competent to prosecute any disciplinary infringement ex officio , as established in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 9.8 If  any of the formal conditions of a protest as set out in these Regulations (and in the FIFA Disciplinary Code, where applicable) are not met, such protest shall be disregarded by the competent body. Once the final match of the FIFA World Cup 26 has ended, any protests described in this article shall be disregarded. Notwithstanding the above, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee remains competent to prosecute any disciplinary infringement ex officio , as established in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.9 FIFA shall pass decisions on any protests lodged, subject to the exceptions stipulated in these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes or any other FIFA regulations.
texts.text: 9.9 FIFA shall pass decisions on any protests lodged, subject to the exceptions stipulated in these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes or any other FIFA regulations.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 10:  YELLOW AND RED CARDS
texts.text: ARTICLE 10:  YELLOW AND RED CARDS

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.1 The referee has the authority to take disciplinary action from entering the field of play for the pre-match inspection until leaving the field of play after the match ends (including penalties (penalty shoot-out)). If, before entering the field of play at the start of the match, a player or team official commits a sending-off offence, the referee has the authority to prevent the player or team official from taking part in the match (see Laws of the Game, Law 12.3, or any relevant amendment); the referee will report any other misconduct.
texts.text: 10.1 The referee has the authority to take disciplinary action from entering the field of play for the pre-match inspection until leaving the field of play after the match ends (including penalties (penalty shoot-out)). If, before entering the field of play at the start of the match, a player or team official commits a sending-off offence, the referee has the authority to prevent the player or team official from taking part in the match (see Laws of the Game, Law 12.3, or any relevant amendment); the referee will report any other misconduct.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.2 Single yellow cards and pending suspensions as a consequence of cautions in different matches in the preliminary competition are not carried over to the final competition. Pending match suspensions imposed as a result of a red card in matches in the preliminary competition are carried over to the final competition.
texts.text: 10.2 Single yellow cards and pending suspensions as a consequence of cautions in different matches in the preliminary competition are not carried over to the final competition. Pending match suspensions imposed as a result of a red card in matches in the preliminary competition are carried over to the final competition.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.3 Single yellow cards in the final competition will be cancelled after the quarter-finals.
texts.text: 10.3 Single yellow cards in the final competition will be cancelled after the quarter-finals.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.4 If a player or team official receives two cautions during two different matches, they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match.
texts.text: 10.4 If a player or team official receives two cautions during two different matches, they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.5 If a player or team official is sent off as a result of a direct or indirect red card (second caution), they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match. In addition, further sanctions may be imposed.
texts.text: 10.5 If a player or team official is sent off as a result of a direct or indirect red card (second caution), they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match. In addition, further sanctions may be imposed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.6 Any suspension that cannot be served during the FIFA World Cup 26 will be carried over to the representative team's next official match in accordance with the relevant provisions of the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 10.6 Any suspension that cannot be served during the FIFA World Cup 26 will be carried over to the representative team's next official match in accordance with the relevant provisions of the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.7 Further details on rules and on the management of suspensions and expulsions will be confirmed via circular letter.
texts.text: 10.7 Further details on rules and on the management of suspensions and expulsions will be confirmed via circular letter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 11:  NUMBER OF TEAMS
texts.text: ARTICLE 11:  NUMBER OF TEAMS

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 11.1 The FIFA Council fixed the number of teams taking part in the FIFA World Cup 26 at 48, namely the host member associations' representative teams of Canada, Mexico and the USA, and the 45 other teams that qualify from the preliminary competition.
texts.text: 11.1 The FIFA Council fixed the number of teams taking part in the FIFA World Cup 26 at 48, namely the host member associations' representative teams of Canada, Mexico and the USA, and the 45 other teams that qualify from the preliminary competition.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 11.2 FIFA shall form groups for the final competition by seeding and drawing lots whilst taking sporting and geographical factors into consideration, as far as possible. Further details will be communicated by circular letter.
texts.text: 11.2 FIFA shall form groups for the final competition by seeding and drawing lots whilst taking sporting and geographical factors into consideration, as far as possible. Further details will be communicated by circular letter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 11.3 The decisions of FIFA on the group formation and the duration of the final competition are final. In the event of any withdrawals or exclusions, FIFA shall decide on the matter at its sole discretion and take whatever action is deemed necessary.
texts.text: 11.3 The decisions of FIFA on the group formation and the duration of the final competition are final. In the event of any withdrawals or exclusions, FIFA shall decide on the matter at its sole discretion and take whatever action is deemed necessary.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 11.4 On 9 May 2017, the FIFA Council decided to allocate the following number of slots to the confederations for the FIFA World Cup 26:
texts.text: 11.4 On 9 May 2017, the FIFA Council decided to allocate the following number of slots to the confederations for the FIFA World Cup 26:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The above slot allocation includes 46 direct slots as well as two slots to be decided in a centralised Play-Off Tournament featuring six teams. The following Play-Off Tournament competition format was approved by the FIFA Council on 9 May 2017:
texts.text: The above slot allocation includes 46 direct slots as well as two slots to be decided in a centralised Play-Off Tournament featuring six teams. The following Play-Off Tournament competition format was approved by the FIFA Council on 9 May 2017:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. One team per confederation (AFC, CAF, CONMEBOL, OFC) with the exception of UEFA, plus two teams from the confederation of the host countries (Concacaf).
texts.text: One team per confederation (AFC, CAF, CONMEBOL, OFC) with the exception of UEFA, plus two teams from the confederation of the host countries (Concacaf).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Two teams to be seeded based on the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking. The two seeded teams will play for a FIFA World Cup 26 slot against the winners of the first two play-off matches involving the four unseeded teams.
texts.text: Two teams to be seeded based on the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking. The two seeded teams will play for a FIFA World Cup 26 slot against the winners of the first two play-off matches involving the four unseeded teams.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On 14 February 2023, the FIFA Council approved the hosts of the FIFA World Cup 26, namely Canada, Mexico and the USA, to qualify automatically for the final competition, with their slots therefore being deducted from the overall allocation of six assigned to Concacaf.
texts.text: On 14 February 2023, the FIFA Council approved the hosts of the FIFA World Cup 26, namely Canada, Mexico and the USA, to qualify automatically for the final competition, with their slots therefore being deducted from the overall allocation of six assigned to Concacaf.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 12:  GROUP AND KNOCKOUT STAGES
texts.text: ARTICLE 12:  GROUP AND KNOCKOUT STAGES

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 12.1 The format of the final competition consists of a group stage, followed by the knockout stages with a round of 32, a round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals, the match for third place and the final.
texts.text: 12.1 The format of the final competition consists of a group stage, followed by the knockout stages with a round of 32, a round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals, the match for third place and the final.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 12.2 The 48 teams taking part in the final competition will be divided into 12 groups of four teams.
texts.text: 12.2 The 48 teams taking part in the final competition will be divided into 12 groups of four teams.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 12.3 FIFA will seed the teams for the Final Draw for the FIFA World Cup 26™ in accordance with the teams' ranking within the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking, as established in the draw procedures. FIFA will divide the teams into groups by seeding and drawing lots in public, taking into account geographical and sporting considerations.
texts.text: 12.3 FIFA will seed the teams for the Final Draw for the FIFA World Cup 26™ in accordance with the teams' ranking within the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking, as established in the draw procedures. FIFA will divide the teams into groups by seeding and drawing lots in public, taking into account geographical and sporting considerations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As the host countries, Canada, Mexico and the USA will be assigned to the following positions: A1 for Mexico, B1 for Canada and D1 for the USA, as per the match schedule released on 4 February 2024 and as applied in the draw procedures.
texts.text: As the host countries, Canada, Mexico and the USA will be assigned to the following positions: A1 for Mexico, B1 for Canada and D1 for the USA, as per the match schedule released on 4 February 2024 and as applied in the draw procedures.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The teams in the 12 groups will be designated as follows:
texts.text: The teams in the 12 groups will be designated as follows:

texts.text: Competition format III  .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 12.4 The group matches will be played following the schedule below. Each team plays each of the other teams in the same group once, according to a league system with three points for a win, one point for a draw and no points for a defeat.
texts.text: 12.4 The group matches will be played following the schedule below. Each team plays each of the other teams in the same group once, according to a league system with three points for a win, one point for a draw and no points for a defeat.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note: the above configuration does not necessarily represent the chronological order in which the matches will be played.
texts.text: Note: the above configuration does not necessarily represent the chronological order in which the matches will be played.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The last two matches in each group shall have simultaneous kick-off times on the same day, unless stipulated otherwise by FIFA (e.g. in cases of force majeure).
texts.text: The last two matches in each group shall have simultaneous kick-off times on the same day, unless stipulated otherwise by FIFA (e.g. in cases of force majeure).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 12.5 The 12 teams finishing first and second in each group as well as the eight best teams among those finishing third will qualify for the round of 32.
texts.text: 12.5 The 12 teams finishing first and second in each group as well as the eight best teams among those finishing third will qualify for the round of 32.

texts.text: 12.6 The 32 teams that qualify from the group stage will contest the round of 32 as follows:

texts.text: Note: the above configuration of matches for the round of 32 does not necessarily represent the chronological order in which the matches will be played as teams from the same group shall not meet each other in the round of 32.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Annexe C of these Regulations includes the 495 different possible combinations of the eight best-ranked third-placed teams and their next match up for the round of 32, at the completion of the group stage.
texts.text: Annexe C of these Regulations includes the 495 different possible combinations of the eight best-ranked third-placed teams and their next match up for the round of 32, at the completion of the group stage.

texts.orig: 12.7 The 16 teams that qualify from the round of 32 will contest the round of 16 as follows:
texts.text: 12.7 The 16 teams that qualify from the round of 32 will contest the round of 16 as follows:

texts.text: 12.10 The two runners-up of the semi-finals will contest the play-off for third place, as follows:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 13:  EQUAL POINTS AND QUALIFICATION FOR KNOCKOUT STAGES
texts.text: ARTICLE 13:  EQUAL POINTS AND QUALIFICATION FOR KNOCKOUT STAGES

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If two or more teams in the same group are equal on points after the completion of the group stage, the following criteria, in the order below, shall be applied to determine the ranking:
texts.text: If two or more teams in the same group are equal on points after the completion of the group stage, the following criteria, in the order below, shall be applied to determine the ranking:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Step 1:
texts.text: Step 1:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  greatest number of points obtained in the group matches between the teams concerned;
texts.text: greatest number of points obtained in the group matches between the teams concerned;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  superior goal difference resulting from the group matches between the teams concerned;
texts.text: superior goal difference resulting from the group matches between the teams concerned;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  greatest number of goals scored in all group matches between the teams concerned.
texts.text: greatest number of goals scored in all group matches between the teams concerned.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Step 2:
texts.text: Step 2:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If, after having applied criteria a) to c) above, teams still have an equal ranking, and in order to determine their final rankings, criteria a) to c) above are applied to the matches between the remaining teams only. If no decision can be made through this procedure, criteria d) to f) below shall apply as follows to the two or more teams still equal on points:
texts.text: If, after having applied criteria a) to c) above, teams still have an equal ranking, and in order to determine their final rankings, criteria a) to c) above are applied to the matches between the remaining teams only. If no decision can be made through this procedure, criteria d) to f) below shall apply as follows to the two or more teams still equal on points:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d)  superior goal difference in all group matches;
texts.text: superior goal difference in all group matches;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e)  greatest number of goals scored in all group matches;
texts.text: greatest number of goals scored in all group matches;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f) highest team conduct score (players and team officials) relating to the number of yellow and red cards obtained:
texts.text: highest team conduct score (players and team officials) relating to the number of yellow and red cards obtained:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Only one of the above deductions shall be applied to a player or team official in a single match. The team with the highest number of points shall be ranked highest.
texts.text: Only one of the above deductions shall be applied to a player or team official in a single match. The team with the highest number of points shall be ranked highest.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With respect to the second step, all affected teams will be ranked by applying the criteria d) to f) in order. If one team qualifies for a higher or lower ranking pursuant to one criterion, but it is not possible to rank all teams on the basis of the same criterion, the remaining two or three teams will be ranked pursuant to the next criterion, and so on. In any case, the second step of the ranking does not restart for the two or three teams remaining after application of a criterion.
texts.text: With respect to the second step, all affected teams will be ranked by applying the criteria d) to f) in order. If one team qualifies for a higher or lower ranking pursuant to one criterion, but it is not possible to rank all teams on the basis of the same criterion, the remaining two or three teams will be ranked pursuant to the next criterion, and so on. In any case, the second step of the ranking does not restart for the two or three teams remaining after application of a criterion.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Step 3:
texts.text: Step 3:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If no decision can be made through the procedures of step 1 and 2, the following shall apply:
texts.text: If no decision can be made through the procedures of step 1 and 2, the following shall apply:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: g)  the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the most recent published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking;
texts.text: the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the most recent published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: h)  the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking immediately preceding the most recent published edition continually until a decision can be made.
texts.text: the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking immediately preceding the most recent published edition continually until a decision can be made.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: The eight best-ranked teams among those finishing third (third-placed teams from 1 to 8)
texts.text: The eight best-ranked teams among those finishing third (third-placed teams from 1 to 8)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The eight best teams among those ranked third will be determined as follows:
texts.text: The eight best teams among those ranked third will be determined as follows:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  greatest number of points obtained in all group matches;
texts.text: greatest number of points obtained in all group matches;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  goal difference resulting from all group matches;
texts.text: goal difference resulting from all group matches;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  greatest number of goals scored in all group matches;
texts.text: greatest number of goals scored in all group matches;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d)  highest team conduct score (players and team officials) relating to the number of yellow and red cards obtained in all group matches and calculated as per article 13 paragraph 1, step 2;
texts.text: highest team conduct score (players and team officials) relating to the number of yellow and red cards obtained in all group matches and calculated as per article 13 paragraph 1, step 2;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e)  the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the most recent published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking;
texts.text: the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the most recent published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f) the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking immediately preceding the most recent published edition continually until a decision can be made.
texts.text: the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking immediately preceding the most recent published edition continually until a decision can be made.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ARTICLE 14:    EXTRA TIME AND PENALTIES (PENALTY SHOOT-OUT)
texts.text: ARTICLE 14:    EXTRA TIME AND PENALTIES (PENALTY SHOOT-OUT)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 14.1 In the knockout stages, if a match is level at the end of normal playing time, extra time shall be played. Extra time shall consist of two 15-minute periods, with an interval of five minutes at the end of normal playing time, but not between the two periods of extra time. The players shall remain on the pitch during both of these intervals.
texts.text: 14.1 In the knockout stages, if a match is level at the end of normal playing time, extra time shall be played. Extra time shall consist of two 15-minute periods, with an interval of five minutes at the end of normal playing time, but not between the two periods of extra time. The players shall remain on the pitch during both of these intervals.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 14.2 If the score is still level at the end of extra time, penalties (penalty shoot-out) shall be taken to determine the winner, in accordance with the procedure specified in the Laws of the Game.
texts.text: 14.2 If the score is still level at the end of extra time, penalties (penalty shoot-out) shall be taken to determine the winner, in accordance with the procedure specified in the Laws of the Game.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 14.3 Before the start of a penalty shoot-out to determine the outcome of a match:
texts.text: 14.3 Before the start of a penalty shoot-out to determine the outcome of a match:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a)  unless there are other considerations (e.g. ground conditions, safety, etc.), the referee tosses a coin to decide the goal at which the kicks will be taken, which may only be changed for safety reasons or if the goal or playing surface becomes unusable; and
texts.text: unless there are other considerations (e.g. ground conditions, safety, etc.), the referee tosses a coin to decide the goal at which the kicks will be taken, which may only be changed for safety reasons or if the goal or playing surface becomes unusable; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  the referee tosses a coin again, and the team that wins the toss decides whether to take the first or second kick.
texts.text: the referee tosses a coin again, and the team that wins the toss decides whether to take the first or second kick.
